items.title: Paes over impact blessures Jaros en Zinchenko op Ajax: 'Zijn speech maakte indruk'
items.summary: Maarten Paes is de nieuwe eerste doelman van Ajax na de blessure van Vitezslav Jaros, die na Oleksandr Zinchenko de volgende speler is die dit seizoen niet meer in actie komt in Amsterdam. De keeper vertelt over de impact van het ontbreken van zijn teamgenoten.

items.title: Man City's 'massive' win piles pressure on Arsenal
items.summary: Pep Guardiola hailed a "massive" win after title-chasing Manchester City held on for a crucial yet nervy victory over Newcastle on Saturday.

items.title: Klopp: Liverpool success impossible without Milner
items.summary: Jürgen Klopp has said that James Milner's achievement of breaking the Premier League appearance record is unlikely to ever be broken, and praised him for his Liverpool input.

items.title: LAFC down Miami in season opener at Coliseum
items.summary: Inter Miami's defense of its MLS Cup title began in disappointing fashion, with a 3-0 defeat to LAFC at the LA Coliseum on Saturday.

items.title: Burnley, Chelsea condemn racist abuse of players
items.summary: Burnley and Chelsea have condemned online racist abuse directed at their respective players, Hannibal Mejbri and Wesley Fofana, following Saturday's 1-1 draw between the teams.

items.title: Ronaldo nadert 1.000 goals en begraaft strijdbijl: 'Ik hoor bij Saoedi-Arabië'
items.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo heeft zaterdagavond de zoveelste mijlpaal in zijn loopbaan bereikt. De spits van Al-Nassr zette met een dubbelslag bij de 4-0 zege van Al-Nassr op Al-Hazem tevens zijn jacht op duizend officiële goals als professional voort.

items.title: Premier League news, Spurs v Arsenal buildup, Sheffield derby, and more – matchday live
items.summary: ⚽ Buildup to the weekend’s football action ⚽ Follow us over on Bluesky | And mail us here It’s North London Derby day! Always a huge occasion for both teams but it feels even more so today. For Arsenal, today marks a fantastic opportunity to bounce back from their midweek 2-2 draw at Wolves. The result on Wednesday sparked an all too familiar feeling among Arsenal fans as the Gunners lost ground in the title race once again. It meant that Manchester City were able to move within two points following their 2-1 win over Newcastle last night (more on that later). But victory today could spark a much-needed resurgence going into the most important part of the season. Meanwhile, for Tottenham it presents an opportunity to get their first Premier League win of the year. Spurs have endured a run of poor form and now begin a new – albeit likely small – era with new interim head coach Igor Tudor, who was appointed until the end of the season last week following the dismissal of Thomas Frank. Nottingham Forest v Liverpool Sunderland v Fulham Crystal Palace v Wolves Tottenham v Arsenal (4:30pm) Continue reading...

items.title: 'Like landing on the moon': How Milner set a near-unbreakable Premier League record
items.summary: At age 40, James Milner is now the Premier League's all-time appearances leader. Teammates and coaches -- from Ashley Young to Jurgen Klopp -- share what makes him special.

items.title: ‘You need to be a team’: Rowett urges unity as depleted Leicester show fight
items.summary: Gary Rowett has told his Leicester players to deprioritise ‘individual ambitions’ after Saturday’s 2-2 draw with Stoke By the stage Ben Nelson headed against the bar in stoppage time and then Luke Thomas nodded against the inside of a post, it felt as if Leicester City were missing the chance to move into the promotion playoffs, so dominant had they been in the second half of this absorbing 2-2 draw with Stoke City. Yet after this month’s six-point deduction for breaching the Premier League’s profitability and sustainability rules, against which they are appealing, Ben Wilmot’s 89th-minute equaliser dropped Leicester back into the Championship’s relegation zone, placing the challenge facing them and their new manager, Gary Rowett, into stark context. Continue reading...

items.title: NEC krijgt bevestiging in de Arena
items.summary: Met nog tien wedstrijden te gaan dingt NEC mee om een topdrieklassering in de Eredivisie. Zaterdag kreeg trainer Dick Schreuder op bezoek bij Ajax een bevestiging van de ontwikkeling van zijn ploeg, in positieve en in negatieve zin.

items.title: VI Live: Feyenoord sluit Eredivisie-weekend af met late pot tegen Telstar
items.summary: Feyenoord sluit zondag het Eredivisie-weekend af met een thuiswedstrijd tegen Telstar. De twee ploegen staan om 20.00 uur tegenover elkaar. Doet Feyenoord goede zaken in de strijd om plek twee? Je volgt het in deze VI Live.

items.title: Grim ziet verbeterpunten jeugdopleiding Ajax: 'Dat was misschien te makkelijk'
items.summary: Fred Grim kreeg op de persconferentie na het gelijkspel tegen NEC vragen over de jeugdopleiding van Ajax. Wat zou de hoofdtrainer graag zien als hij de technisch directeur mocht adviseren? Daar gaf hij antwoord op.

items.title: Today on Sky Sports Racing: Potters Charm seeks National Spirit gold at Fontwell
items.summary: Potters Charm headlines Sunday afternoon's feature National Spirit Hurdle , live on Sky Sports Racing.

items.title: Rugby League in Las Vegas 2026: Everything you need to know
items.summary: Rugby league once again hits US soil on Saturday February 28 into Sunday March 1 for a thrilling festival of action and Sky Sports is bringing you every game from Sin City.

items.title: ‘Political pressure’ may have influenced decision to ban Maccabi fans, MPs say
items.summary: Local political pressure may have played a part in the decision to ban Maccabi Tel Aviv football fans from attending a match against Aston Villa, a committee of MPs has concluded.

items.title: ‘Caipirinhas, daiquiris’: Guardiola tells City players to chill and ignore title pressure
items.summary: Manchester City beat Newcastle to cut Arsenal’s lead to two Team given three days off before facing Leeds Pep Guardiola told his players to “take a lot of caipirinhas, daiquiris” to handle the pressure of the title chase after Manchester City beat Newcastle 2-1 to close to within two points of Arsenal. Two goals from Nico O’Reilly on 14 and 27 minutes at the Etihad Stadium were answered only by Lewis Hall’s strike in between as City moved to 56 points and a goal difference of 31, one fewer than Arsenal’s. Continue reading...

items.title: Chelsea’s Fofana and Burnley’s Hannibal abused by online racists
items.summary: Fofana’s first of two bookings was for fouling Hannibal Burnley: ‘There is no place for this in our society’ Hannibal Mejbri and Wesley Fofana have both been racially abused on Instagram in the wake of the former’s Burnley side drawing 1-1 at Chelsea after the latter was sent off. Hannibal, who was fouled for the first of the two yellow cards that led to Fofana’s dismissal, posted the abuse he had received via a direct message on Instagram and wrote: “It’s 2026 and there still ppl like that … Educate yourself and your kids pls.” Fofana likewise posted the abuse he received. Continue reading...

items.title: O'Reilly scores twice as Man City beat Newcastle
items.summary: Manchester City move to within two points of Premier League leaders Arsenal with a tense victory against Newcastle United at Etihad Stadium.

items.title: Pep tells City players: Drink cocktails and enjoy life after 'massive' win
items.summary: Pep Guardiola does not expect Man City to stay perfect in the Premier League title race - despite claiming what he described as a "massive" win over Newcastle that moves them two points behind leaders Arsenal.

items.title: Ten-man Chelsea drop points again as Burnley score stoppage-time equaliser
items.summary: Chelsea make it 17 points dropped from winning positions in the Premier League this season as Zian Flemming's 93rd-minute header gives Burnley a 1-1 draw, after Wesley Fofana is sent off at Stamford Bridge.
